_provenance: Prod stored-course FeatureCollection for Pebble Beach Golf Links hole 3 (par 4, ~381y derived) — captured read-only 2026-07-18 for the all-courses tee-selector audit (specs/caddie-yardage-selector-p0-plan.md Lead 2). A second Class A bogus-bend-cap conviction: mapped corner at ~265y, but the nearest qualifying tree under the OLD (unbounded) filter sits 85y past the corner (essentially at the green). BEFORE the fix this capped the drive off a 4-iron; AFTER the CORNER_TREE_FORWARD_YDS=40 bound, it no longer qualifies and the drive is uncapped.

green_slope.description: Green slopes severely toward the southeast

strategy_guide.miss_side: If you're going to miss, miss left — the right side is loaded with sand from 265 out to 300.
strategy_guide.play_line: From the tee, favor the left-center of the fairway to stay away from the string of bunkers guarding the right side while still clearing the pair of bunkers sitting center at 205 and 230.
strategy_guide.green_notes: The green tips hard to the southeast, so plan your approach and any putts to work with that severe slope rather than fight it.
common_mistakes: Drifting the tee shot right into one of the 265-300y bunkers
Laying up into the center bunkers at 205-230 instead of picking a line past them
Misjudging putts and short chips by ignoring the severe southeast fall of the green
